exemplar.title: Exploring sound, frequencies and their impact on the human ear

detailedExemplarData.title: Exploring sound, frequencies and their impact on the human ear

strengths: Time-blocking calendar provides compelling evidence of effective self-management and scheduling to balance commitments.
Detailed reflection on self-management ATL skillsâorganization, time and stress management, accountabilityâshowing clear understanding of how these skills supported progress.
CARRDS evaluation table offers rigorous source analysis with detailed justifications of credibility, accuracy, relevance and currency.
Research question table clearly outlines primary and complementary questions, demonstrating a thorough research plan that underpins product development.
weaknesses: Research findings are described but not explicitly linked to specific improvements in the videoâs content or design.
The impact of ATL skills on the final product quality could be strengthened by providing direct examples of how these skills elevated specific elements of the video.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The student shows strong application of ATL skills, particularly in self-management and research evaluation. To deepen this analysis, they should map individual research insights and ATL applications directly onto product enhancements, illustrating how each skill contributed to identifiable improvements in the video.

strengths: Content and production planning table breaks down scripting, visuals, audio and revision phases aligned with success criteria, demonstrating strong project management.
Excellent description of how lifelong interest in environmental factors and classroom introduction to sound waves led to the learning goal, giving clear personal context.
Well-structured and measurable product success criteria using surveys with numeric thresholds and source requirements to evaluate content accuracy and credibility.
Detailed timeline with clear dates, tasks, progress notes and linked success criteria, showing effective sequencing and resource planning.
Comprehensive multi-phase plan from research and familiarization to production and revision, demonstrating a realistic approach to achieving the product goal.
weaknesses: Learning goal is defined but lacks clear, measurable indicators of progress in video creation (e.g., milestones or mini-deliverables).
Intended product statement is clear but missing associated success criteria such as specific measures for number of animations or confirmed video length.
Some success criteria are stated generally; more precise, time-bound metrics would strengthen the plan.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The planning demonstrates a high level of organization and depth, with clear breakdowns of tasks, timelines and resource needs. To enhance this strength, the student should define measurable checkpoints for the learning goal and product outcome, ensuring each phase can be assessed against specific, time-bound criteria.

strengths: Thorough evaluation of both learning and product against multiple success criteria, with concrete examples and evidence of skill development.
Insightful reflection on IB learner profile traits and discussion of how project experiences will inform future work in the Diploma Programme.
Strong quantitative evidence from engagement, information relevance and visual professionalism surveys, demonstrating that the product met audience expectations.
One-point rubric table provides a concise and clear evaluation of product success criteria with supporting evidence.
weaknesses: Reflection notes that transitions felt uneven but lacks detailed strategies (e.g., specific linking sentences or visual cues) to improve narrative flow.
Proposed improvements (broader survey, original visuals) are promising but missing a concrete timeline and resource plan for implementation in the next cycle.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The reflection is well-developed, with robust evidence and thoughtful consideration of personal growth and future applications. To build on this, the student should incorporate targeted plans for smoother transitions and outline a clear schedule and resources for implementing proposed enhancements.

annotations.comment: Your content and production planning table breaks down scripting, visual, audio and revision phases clearly, aligning each task with success criteria and demonstrating strong project management.

Youâve defined a learning goal but havenât specified how to measure your progress in video creation. Add clear, measurable success criteria (e.g., complete a tutorial and produce a 1-minute edited clip by date X).
Excellent description of how your lifelong interest in environmental factors and classroom introduction to sound waves led to your learning goal, giving clear personal context.
Your intended product statement is clear but missing associated success criteria. Define specific measures (e.g., number of animations created, video length confirmed) to assess your product goal.
Your product success criteria are well structured and measurable, using surveys with numeric thresholds and source requirements to evaluate content accuracy and credibility.
Youâve outlined a logical narrative flow and research plan, including survey design and transition guidelines, which strengthens the feasibility of your product structure.
Your timeline is detailed with clear dates, tasks, progress notes and linked success criteria, demonstrating strong sequencing and resource planning for project completion.
The multi-phase plan from research and familiarization to production and revision shows a comprehensive and realistic approach to achieving your product goal.
The time-blocking calendar screenshot is compelling evidence of effective self-management, illustrating how you structured your day to balance multiple commitments.
Your research question table clearly outlines primary and complementary questions, demonstrating a thorough research plan that supports your product development.
You describe a variety of sources but could strengthen your reflection by explicitly linking each research finding to specific improvements in your videoâs content or design.
You provide a detailed reflection on self-management skills, including organization, time and stress management, and accountability, showing clear understanding of ATL skill application.
The CARRDS evaluation table shows rigorous source analysis, providing detailed justification of credibility, accuracy, relevance and currency for a key article.
You evaluate your learning and product against success criteria, providing evidence of skill development and demonstrating critical reflection on your growth as a learner.

Your reflection on IB learner profile traits and future application in the Diploma Programme is thorough, showing thoughtful consideration of how project experiences will benefit your academic journey.
You note that transitions felt uneven. Consider mapping out explicit linking sentences or visual cues between sections to ensure smoother narrative flow in future videos.
The one-point rubric table provides a clear, concise evaluation of your product success criteria, highlighting areas of achievement and supporting evidence.
Engagement survey results (18/20 positive) effectively demonstrate that your video achieved its purpose of being engaging and understandable to your target audience.
The survey excerpt for information relevance (20/20 positive) offers strong quantitative evidence that your content met the intended accuracy and comprehensiveness criteria.
The visual professionalism survey (19/20 positive) confirms that your design choices resonated with viewers and met your criteria for professionalism and clarity.
Your proposed improvements (e.g., broader survey, original visuals) are promising; add a concrete timeline and resources plan to implement these enhancements in a future cycle.
